name: QuasAr6a
description: Archon1 variant evolved through video-based pooled screening with improved SNR.

spectrum.name: QuasAr6a
spectrum.note: QuasAr6a is an Archon1-derived far-red Arch sensor. The Tian 2023 origin paper shows only phototag/photoactivation spectra, not a clean fluorescence excitation/emission spectrum; the displayed curve is the QuasAr1/QuasAr2 far-red fluorescence spectrum (ex 590 / em 715 nm; Hochbaum 2014, Suppl. Fig. 2d) inherited as the closest measured Arch-family spectrum.

kinetics.note: Amplitude-weighted mean from biexponential fit at 30°C. Activation: 90% fast tau 1.8 ms, 10% slow tau 6.3 ms. Deactivation: 88% fast tau 1.3 ms, 12% slow tau 4.1 ms.

dynamicRangeData.note: In HEK293T cells, voltage clamp with 635 nm one-photon excitation, per 100 mV step, n=5 cells

sensitivityData.note: Per-spike ΔF/F from high-throughput Optopatch assay in cultured rat hippocampal neurons at room temperature, 635 nm one-photon excitation, highest virus titer, n=843 cells; visually estimated from Fig. 3i, with titer series in Ext. Fig. 5c.

photostabilityData.note: Photobleaching by 635 nm laser at 420 W/cm² (= 4200 mW/mm²) over 10 min in HEK293T cells, n=2 cells. Caption states all constructs showed <40% photobleaching over 10 min; QuasAr6a trace ends at ~62% remaining. (Main text mis-cites this as Ext. Fig. 6f, but the photobleach panel is Ext. Fig. 4f.)

apWidthData.sample: Mouse, visual cortex layer 1 NDNF interneurons, in vivo
apWidthData.note: Optical spike FWHM (full width at half-maximum of the optically-recorded single AP) of optogenetically triggered spikes in somQuasAr6a-expressing NDNF cells in vivo, imaged at a 1 kHz (996 Hz) frame rate. Read as the box-plot median in Fig. 4d (~2.4 ms; box ~2.2-2.65 ms, n=32 cells, two animals). This is a true FWHM and is distinct from the 'optical spike width' of 10.4 ms in Fig. 3h, which is defined as the full width at 80% below the AP peak (a much wider metric, not comparable to FWHM). The paper prints numeric FWHM only for QuasAr6b (0.87 ms) and Archon1 (1.1 ms) in PV cells at 2 kHz (Fig. 4h); QuasAr6a was measured only in NDNF cells (Fig. 4d), so its value is the panel median. Frame rate 1 kHz recorded; body temperature (~37°C) in vivo.

photobleach.note: Photobleaching re-digitized from Ext. Fig. 4f (QuasAr6a-Citrine, red curve), HEK293T, continuous 635-nm laser at 420 W/cm² (= 4200 mW/mm²), 10 min, n=2 cells. Mono-exponential fit (tau≈1549 s, R²=0.96), t75≈445 s; QuasAr6a is the lowest of the three curves, ending at ~62-66% of F0 at 585-600 s (consistent with photostabilityData 62%). NOT dose-normalized to 100 mW/mm² (intensityMWmm2 omitted): this is an opsin imaged at extreme intensity where bleaching is not dose-linear and the sensor is never used near 100 mW/mm². Measured alongside Archon1 and QuasAr6b in the same figure → cross-overlay (see quasar6b/archon1 entries). Re-digitized 2026-07 because the prior trace over-read the red curve (endpoint ~70%); the corrected trace hugs the red data and matches the figure endpoint.
